## Claims

1. Added [polyglot] optional dep group with tree-sitter, tree-sitter-javascript, tree-sitter-typescript; also added to [dev] group
2. No existing tests were modified or deleted during this change.
3. P1-6/7/8: LanguageDriver Protocol defines resolve_symbols, build_test_graph, find_downstream_callers. PythonDriver wraps existing ast code. TreeSitterDriver handles JS/JSX/TS/TSX via tree-sitter. Registry auto-discovers drivers at import.
4. Protocol with resolve_symbols, build_test_graph, find_downstream_callers methods. Runtime-checkable for isinstance validation.
5. Delegates to resolve_changed_symbols, build_test_graph, find_downstream_callers in evidence_collector.py
6. Maps file extensions to LanguageDriver instances. PythonDriver always registered. TreeSitterDriver registered lazily when tree-sitter packages available.
7. P1-6/7/8 core: tree-sitter based analysis for .js/.jsx/.mjs/.ts/.tsx files. Handles function declarations, arrow functions, classes with methods, ES6 imports, require(), and call extraction.
8. AST analysis and downstream caller detection now use lang_driver from registry instead of file_posix_raw.endswith('.py'). Enables polyglot evidence for any registered language.
9. Tests cover: registry discovery, PythonDriver delegation, TreeSitterDriver symbol resolution (JS/TS functions, arrow functions, classes, methods, TSX), test graph building, and downstream caller analysis
10. P1-6 polyglot symbol resolution, P1-7 polyglot test graph, P1-8 polyglot downstream callers all implemented via tree-sitter LanguageDriver

## Verification Methodology

**Zero-Touch Mandate:** Verifier inspects artifacts only.
Evidence was collected by `aiv commit` during the change lifecycle.
Packet generated by `aiv close`.

## Known Limitations

- Evidence references point to Layer 1 evidence files at specific commit SHAs.
